mechanism_of_action: Valproate-related amide; precise antiseizure mechanism is not established, with valproate-like effects on GABAergic and ion-channel systems proposed but not definitive.
mechanism_source: NCBI PubChem pharmacology records; Sills and Rogawski 2020 ASM mechanism review

status_or_notes: ASM for infantile spasms and refractory focal/complex partial seizures; restricted because of permanent vision-loss risk.
evidence_sources: NCBI LiverTox anticonvulsants table; Epilepsy Society ASM list; Epilepsy Foundation Australia ASM list; FDA/DailyMed labeling
mechanism_of_action: Precise anti-seizure mechanism is unknown, but effect is believed to result from irreversible inhibition of GABA transaminase, increasing CNS GABA levels.
mechanism_source: FDA/DailyMed labeling
diff_50_responder_maximum_effective_dose: NR/not comparable in extracted placebo-controlled RCT data
diff_median_pct_change_maximum_effective: NR/not comparable in extracted placebo-controlled RCT data
diff_seizure_freedom_maximum_effective_d: 25 % (drug minus placebo seizure-freedom differential at maximum reported active RCT dose/regimen: Appleton1999 vigabatrin first-line infantile-spasm treatment 25%)
typical_doses_per_day: Adults refractory focal seizures: 1000-3000 mg/day divided BID; infantile spasms weight-based
minimum_effective_dose: 1000 mg/day adults; 50 mg/kg/day infantile spasms start
enzyme_inducing_or_inhibiting: Not significantly metabolized; not an enzyme inducer/inhibitor
qt_interval_effect: No clinically meaningful QT effect established
adverse_symptoms_percentages: CNS: somnolence 24%, constitutional: fatigue 24%, ophthalmologic: visual field loss 30% or more with chronic exposure, CNS: dizziness 12%, metabolic: weight gain 10%, neurologic: tremor 8%

status_or_notes: Current ASM for focal/partial seizures; also used off-label for migraine/weight-related indications in some settings.
evidence_sources: NCBI LiverTox anticonvulsants table; Epilepsy Society ASM list; Epilepsy Foundation Australia ASM list; FDA/DailyMed labeling
mechanism_of_action: Precise mechanism is unknown; in vitro data suggest sodium-channel blockade and reduced T-type calcium currents, with weak carbonic-anhydrase inhibition of uncertain therapeutic contribution.
mechanism_source: FDA/DailyMed labeling
diff_50_responder_maximum_effective_dose: NR/not comparable in extracted placebo-controlled RCT data
diff_median_pct_change_maximum_effective: NR/not comparable in extracted placebo-controlled RCT data
diff_seizure_freedom_maximum_effective_d: NR/not extractable as a drug-minus-placebo seizure-freedom patient-rate differential from included phase II/III placebo-controlled RCT records
typical_doses_per_day: Adults: 100-400 mg/day once daily or divided
minimum_effective_dose: 100 mg/day starting; effective maintenance commonly 100-400 mg/day
enzyme_inducing_or_inhibiting: Not a major inducer/inhibitor; CYP3A4 substrate; weak carbonic anhydrase inhibitor
qt_interval_effect: No clinically meaningful QT effect established
adverse_symptoms_percentages: CNS: somnolence 17%, GI: anorexia 13%, CNS: dizziness 13%, neurologic: ataxia 6%, cognitive: abnormal thinking 6%, renal/metabolic: kidney stones 4% over development program
